scenario_title: Delete a nonexistent RR with satisfied prerequisites

group_1.behavior: Treat the absent exact RR in delete_rr as a no-op once the prerequisite rrset_exists succeeds; return success and leave the surviving RRset intact.

group_2.behavior: Fail the UPDATE when the exact RR to delete is absent even though the enclosing rrset exists; the existing RRset is left unchanged because the update is rejected.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2 and §3.4.2.6 define delete_rr syntax and the prerequisite/update split, but do not state whether deleting a specific RR that is absent from an existing RRset should succeed as a no-op or fail with NXRRSET once rrset_exists has already passed. RFC 2181 §5 clarifies RRset semantics, but not the exact UPDATE error mapping for this case.
expected_divergence.test_rationale: The zone contains exactly one A RR at n.campus.edu. The UPDATE first proves the RRset exists, then issues delete_rr for a different A RR at the same owner name. This isolates the underspecified question of whether the server treats deletion of a nonexistent RR as success or as an RRset-level semantic error, while the follow-up QUERY confirms the surviving A RR remains in place either way.
